Yeah, this one.
The other one.

Can into my note from this top and this top?

then also, so I want, it felt different than all the others.
I'm not sure what, maybe it was a different writer or something.
But.
Yeah.
I wanted to, like, I wanted to note that for you.
Okay, okay, thanks.
No, it was the same person.
What sometimes happens, and this is, I think it's just a matter of the subject matter.
Yeah.
think when the topics get into data privacy and security, the research itself is, the subject matter is quite serious.
And I think the AI kind of mimics, like, the.
But the.
The bulk of the research that's available, like all the information that's available, and the security, like cybersecurity content tends to be a bit more serious.
So I think, yeah, we can definitely adjust the tone throughout.
And this is something that we've done for a different client.
They are a data infrastructure platform, and they also do a lot of content around cybersecurity.
And one interesting thing is that when we told it to be more conversational, it can sometimes make certain conclusions that kind of stretch the truth a little bit.
I think the AI, in its quest to be more conversational, tries to make assumptions that sometimes aren't quite true.
And that's something that our human editors pick up on.
So we actually, we're just talking about that today.
So what they
did was once they generated the final draft, they would just kind of run a final tone check and try to pinpoint any sections where the tone is off and like too severe.
And I do agree that like leading with the risk and like misconfigured permissions is kind of like setting it up in a doom and gloom way.
Like it's better to say like, hey, these tools are really beneficial.
However, like you should still watch out for these things.
So it's just about the way you frame it and then also like making sure the tone isn't like stiff.
Yes.
So I agree.
And that's what I was pointing out.

No, it's not.

So then in that case, my feedback in this is – So see.
is valid, and I'd like it to be addressed, and I have not finalized my feedback for you yet, because I'm having our privacy expert look at this article and the other, but that's where I'm at.

All right, so once we finalize the feedback here, we can go.
Hopefully, I'd like to have that finalized for you tomorrow or Thursday.
I'm working as quickly as I can, and he got back to me today, so I'd like to look at what he had to say, and then I think there's only one more that I have not yet looked at.
Yes, correct.
Okay, so we're on pace, yeah?
Yeah, yeah, totally.
The two that are finalized from last week, so the AI Meeting Coach one and How to Have Fewer Meetings, those are ready to publish, and I'm going to publish them early tomorrow when I start working.

you.
Thank

Good.

This allows your content, anything that you publish on your website gets indexed quite quickly, but we also want to make sure that, like, but there's also another component to indexing, which is how in-depth a piece of content is.
So very thin content or content that's kind of, like, superficial will be ignored by Google because it's not helpful to searchers.

So when the bots are crawling your website, they're also kind of assessing whether a page meets the search intent for specific keywords and whether, like, it's valuable enough to be served in search results.
So the fact that those four got indexed pretty quickly is great.
And they're already getting- That show, sorry, can you show me where you see that?

Yes.

the performance of our content.

Wait, wait.

I get access?
Yes.

I have to, let's see, switch different accounts, switch accounts.
Let's see.

Okay, cool.

direct traffic, like, anytime you click.

And we can see the clusters here.

How many clicks.

which is good.

So Google shows 10 results per page.
Okay.
Yeah.

Is that the term?

Yeah, that's a good.

So each of the blogs in this, in the, in the cluster there, you can see their primary keyword is

So like knowledge management strategy, that's our primary keyword.

Because isn't each blog post under each topic cluster its own keyword, has its own keyword goal?

Blog post, like knowledge management strategy guide.
The target keyword is knowledge management strategy, but it's also going to rank for probably more than a dozen other related keywords.
So we want to rank for the primary keyword, but anything else that we can capture along the way is great.
So those would be the first signals, which are really positive, and that's what we typically see.
And then as we continue publishing, we're, you know, this is like, it takes about like four to six weeks to really see the impact of a blog post.
And, you know, in the beginning, like Google might rank it in different positions to see how it performs, like what's the click-through rate.
So, you know, it's still pretty, yeah.
So we'll see like how the traffic changes over the next like couple of weeks.
But overall, this is, this is positive.
Great.
I'd love to see a new.
column with the article so that as we have more articles, it's clear which one we're talking about.
Yeah, yeah, totally.
No, I agree with you.
I think we can add that pretty easily.
All right.
And then for, yeah, so I am working on preparing a phase one summary for you with the benchmarks.
I want to have that ready by the end of this week.
And then for the other action item from our meeting on Friday.
So we talked about adding like an umbrella topic over AI agents.
So what I found to be a suitable topic is AI in the workplace.
Let me just go back to our content OS.
So, yeah, so AI agents was envisioned to be the hub of one of the hubs of AI and automation education.
And I think if.
If we were to move AI agents as a spoke, and then in that case, a good new hub would be AI in the workplace.
So here we could talk about just AI in general and workplace automation, and then mention AI agents in the content.
So I think that allows it, it's a more evergreen topic than AI agents.
Yes, I agree.
Okay, so let's move this.
I'll just add this as a reminder to myself.
All right.
Cool.
Did you have any other questions?
I think you want to- I just have one other thing that's top of mind.
Okay.
Which is, and maybe we can look in the notion for a second, which is our focus on sales.
Sales.
Sales.
Sales.
We'll see you next time.
Teams, and that's how we're addressing AI automation in the sales funnel.
Okay.
I believe that we already have content planned for that in your outline, but I just want to confirm again that we do.
I know that we do.
Let me just, yeah, for recruiting.
Okay, we have AI for sales.
Yeah.
Let me just see this book.
So that is a hub.
Yeah.
Sales teams, great.
Coaching, sales handoff best practices.
How does AI can use?
How do you know?
Yeah, I think one other, like, I don't have the right words for it in this exact moment, but we use reasoning and memory, mostly reasoning, but memories included in it, to offer suggestions.
Okay.
And so I want to be sure that we have some piece of content that speaks to the power of reasoning and insight and driving and closing deals faster.
Mm hmm.
And I have some data on what we have, like on our product, but also I just think like the power of using AI to move through the funnel faster is a powerful concept and we should add it.
Mm hmm.
For sure.
Anything like specifically that you would like to call out?
we have something called sales AGI, which is like artificial general intelligence.
So I would like to call that out as being a tool for teams to use.
That's self-serving.
And I don't know if there's like sales with search volume across that.
So like anything that gets to that point.
Interesting.
Okay.
Yeah, I think we can go and look at like the specific, we can look at benefits of sales AGI and we can also look at like pain points that sales AGI solves and then do keyword research around that.
Yeah.
like we're trying, it's like, you know, in that movie it's like trying to make Fetch a thing.
Like we're trying to make sales AGI a thing.
Like it's not really a thing.
So defer to you on whether you want to call like in your search, you want to, it's actually sales AGI is not a powerful search term.
Maybe it is, but maybe it's more of sales automation or like AI CRMs or that's not, would be AI CRMs, but AI integrations that move deals.
forward or like tools I need as a sales leader or however you wanted to, you know?
Yeah, yeah, totally.
I think we've, yeah, there's a ton of, I have from like previous client work, I already know there's a lot to talk about in terms of AI and sales.
And we've done like a lot of our clients, they do come to us with concepts of like, we're trying to create this industry term or like this approach.
So, we do weave it into the content as like a solution to whatever like issue that we're talking about.
So, yeah, we can definitely do this.
Okay.
If we were successful in this, that would be a very big success for us.
Yeah, for sure.
I think with, we can also use like these screenshots to kind of make the content more visually engaging.
That's helpful.
Okay, so I'll look into just adding more sales-related keywords.
All right.
Cool.
Are we going to have, like, for this pilot phase, is it only 10 pieces of content or am I going to get more?
You're going to get more.
So I think it's around, like, 19, 20.
Okay.
Yeah, yeah.
So it's going to be, like, five per week for the rest of the engagement.
Okay, that's what I thought.
Great.
Yeah, yeah.
All right, so.
Is this pace okay with you?

I met with Tyler yesterday, and I want to be sure, like, he was, at first he thought maybe we were off pace, but then I was like, no, I think we're, like, I've been responding to you and your content creation as quickly as I can.
I think we're, are we good, according to you?
Yes, we are.
Yeah, yeah, yeah, for sure.
Yeah, we are right now in week six.
So we published in week five.
We're not behind production.
So, no, we're definitely, like, on track.
Great.
Yeah.
I think where typically where the bumps happen is whenever there are multiple reviewers, there is always the risk of like delays, but that hasn't been the case here.
So you, you're really like on top of it.
But yeah, whenever there's multiple reviewers in a loop, there's always like that risk of, you know.
I agree.
Well, don't hold your tongue because I have a review.
I have a privacy guy looking at stuff now.
Okay.
Okay, good.
Actually, that's a question for you.
And maybe you're going to deflect that question back to me.
But there's a few things that the articles recommend that we're not quite doing yet.
My inclination would be to soften the suggestion in your article because we're not delivering it against it.
Is do you agree with that approach?
That's fine.
That's Okay.
As long as we mention it and talk about it, it's fine.
How hard we recommend it, we can adjust it.
Okay.
The issue is when, like, sometimes, like,

The bigger issue is when, and this is not so much the case at growthx, but I remember my first agency job and like my very first client, they were a fintech company and they were very, like, they had a very specific product.
And we would have to target like different types of keywords that are related to their product, but not like super specific.
And we have to cover, like, if you want to rank for a specific keyword, you have to cover that topic comprehensively.
And we would get reviews like, why are we talking about all of these things?
We should just be talking about our product.
And it's, the issue is in cutting sections, not so much like changing the angle or like the tone.
So as long as we keep that information in, it's fine.
Just keep it information, so you can't cut the whole thing, but just soften it and adjust.
Okay.
Yeah, yeah, exactly.
I mean, if you really, if there is a section where you're like, I really don't want us to talk about it, like, let us know.
But I think, you know, if.
it's something that's like really crucial for the search intent, like if we're writing a listicle about like how to do something and we miss a very important step, then that could be like an issue.
But if it's like removing one of the questions from the frequently asked questions section, that's fine.
Okay.
I think it's somewhere in between those two examples, but I will let you know and tag you if I have any real questions about it.
Okay.
Okay.
Cool.
That works.
Okay, great.
Thanks, Aida.
All right.
Thank you.
I'll see you next week.
Bye-bye.
Okay.
Thanks.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content review, performance, and strategy adjustments.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=34.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Content Tone Correction:** The security/privacy article's "doom and gloom" tone must be softened. This is a stylistic choice, as a positive frame is equally effective for SEO.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=255.0)
  - [**Strong Initial Performance:** The first four articles published last week are already indexed and ranking on page one (avg. position 7–10), a direct result of read.ai's high domain authority (44/100).](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [**Strategy Pivot:** The "AI Agents" hub will be reframed as a spoke under a new, more evergreen "AI in the Workplace" hub to broaden the content's appeal and search potential.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=866.0)
  - [**Sales Content Focus:** The content plan will be expanded to support the "Sales AGI" concept, using keyword research on related pain points (e.g., "AI for sales") to build search volume.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=945.0)

## Topics

### Content Review & Tone

  - [**Problem:** The security/privacy article's tone is overly serious and "doom and gloom."](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=52.0)
  - [**Root Cause:** Aida explained that the AI writer mimics the serious tone of source research on cybersecurity.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=148.0)
  - [**Decision:** The tone must be adjusted to be more conversational.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=191.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This is a stylistic choice; a positive frame (e.g., "Here's how to use this tool, but watch out for...") is equally effective for SEO.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=272.0)
  - [**Process:** GrowthX will add a final tone-check step to the workflow to prevent this issue.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=235.0)
  - [**Blocker:** Final feedback is pending a review from read.ai's privacy expert, expected by Thursday.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)

### Performance Tracking & Reporting

  - [**Data Source:** Initial performance is tracked in the Looker Report, linked in Notion under "Performance Reports."](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=523.0)
  - [**Key Metric:** The "Content Pillar Click Performance Changes" table (Google Search Console data) shows early ranking signals.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=606.0)
  - [**Findings:**](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
      - [The first four articles are already indexed and receiving impressions.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
      - [They are ranking on page one for some queries (avg. position 7–10).](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=633.0)
      - [**Significance:** This rapid indexing is a direct benefit of read.ai's high domain authority (44/100).](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [**Reporting Improvement:** The report will be updated to show article titles, not just URL slugs, for easier identification.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=737.0)

### Content Strategy Adjustments

  - [**"AI Agents" Hub Pivot:**](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=866.0)
      - [**Decision:** The "AI Agents" hub will become a spoke under a new "AI in the Workplace" hub.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=866.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This creates a more evergreen and broader topic, increasing search potential.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=920.0)
  - [**Sales Content Expansion:**](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=945.0)
      - [**Goal:** Support the "Sales AGI" concept to help sales teams close deals faster.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=1001.0)
      - [**Action:** Add new sales-focused keywords to the content plan.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=1201.0)
      - [**Method:** Research benefits and pain points solved by "Sales AGI" (e.g., "AI for sales," "AI integrations for deals") to build search volume for the new term.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=1086.0)

### Project Pace & Scope

  - [**Pace:** The project is on track.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=1233.0)
      - [**Rationale:** The current pace of \~5 articles/week is sustainable, and Sarah's prompt reviews are preventing delays.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=1233.0)
  - [**Scope:** The pilot phase will deliver more than the initial 10 articles, with a total of \~19–20 planned.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=1211.0)
  - [**Content Edits:**](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=1288.0)
      - [**Decision:** Soften recommendations for features not yet delivered, rather than removing content.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=1288.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This maintains the article's comprehensiveness, which is critical for ranking for its target keyword.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=1317.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Aida:**](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=406.0)
      - [Publish "AI Meeting Coach" and "How to Have Fewer Meetings" tomorrow.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=406.0)
      - [Request an update to the Looker report to show article titles.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=737.0)
      - [Finalize the Phase 1 summary with benchmarks by EOW.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=851.0)
      - [Update the Content OS to reflect the "AI in the Workplace" hub pivot.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=866.0)
      - [Conduct keyword research for sales content, focusing on "Sales AGI" pain points.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=1086.0)
  - [**Sarah:**](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)
      - [Finalize feedback on the security/privacy articles by Thursday.](https://fathom.video/share/u4zpfqZd-R1Csxu5Gh7WqgwZRk2vX5Us?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)

action_items.description: Finalize and send privacy/security article feedback to Aida by Dec 11
Publish AI Meeting Coach and How to Have Fewer Meetings early Dec 10
Ask analytics to add article title column to Looker/GSC Cohorts report
Create 'AI in the workplace' hub; move 'AI agents' under it
Research sales keywords incl Sales AGI; add to Sales Teams hub; plan reasoning/memory piece
